heading: Ban on importing goods and services from countries supporting terrorism
label: 2349aa–9 Ban on importing goods and services from countries supporting terrorism
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the International Security and Development Cooperation Act of 1985, and not as part of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 which comprises this chapter.
Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1985 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1985section 1301 of Pub. L. 99–83section 2151–1 of this title

Delegation of FunctionsFor delegation of functions of President under this section, see Ex. Ord. No. 12163, , 44 F.R. 56673, as amended, set out as a note under .Sept. 29, 1979section 2381 of this title

children.heading: Authority
children.label: a Authority
children.text: The President may ban the importation into the United States of any good or service from any country which supports terrorism or terrorist organizations or harbors terrorists or terrorist organizations.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: b Consultation
children.text: The President, in every possible instance, shall consult with the Congress before exercising the authority granted by this section and shall consult regularly with the Congress so long as that authority is being exercised.
the country with respect to which the authority is to be exercised and the imports to be prohibited;
the circumstances which necessitate the exercise of such authority;
why the President believes those circumstances justify the exercise of such authority; and
why the President believes the prohibitions are necessary to deal with those circumstances.

children.heading: “United States” defined
children.label: d “United States” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “United States” includes territories and possessions of the United States.
